name: Braña de Mumián, Gúa y Caunedo, circular desde Pola de Somiedo — Parque Natural de Somiedo
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Somiedo traverse a landscape characterized by glacial lakes, towering peaks, and deep river valleys within the Cantabrian Mountains. The region features extensive beech forests and rolling meadows, with elevations ranging significantly. Somiedo is recognized as a Natural Park and a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, highlighting its ecological importance. This diverse terrain offers varied hiking experiences for families. Best family friendly hiking trails in Somiedo The most popular family friendly hiking route is Pola de Somiedo – Enramada Beech Forest loop from Somiedo, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through ancient woodlands. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Lago de la Cueva – Lake of the Valley loop from Lagos de Saliencia, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This trail offers views of the Saliencia glacial lakes. Local hikers also love the View of Lago de la Cueva – Lake of the Valley loop from Alto de la Farrapona, an 11.0 miles (17.7 km) trail leading through a landscape of glacial lakes and mountain passes, often completed in about 6 hours 4 minutes. Hiking in Somiedo is defined by glacial lakes, ancient beech forests, and traditional "brañas" (seasonal mountain pastures). The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Somiedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 400 reviews. More than 3,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Somiedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Somiedo?
A: Somiedo offers a wonderful selection of over 30 family-friendly hiking trails. These range from easy strolls perfect for younger children to more moderate adventures for active families, allowing you to explore the park's stunning landscapes at your own pace.

Q: What are some easy family-friendly hikes in Somiedo?
A: While many trails in Somiedo are moderate to difficult, there are a couple of easy routes perfect for families. For a pleasant walk through beautiful woodland, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40304363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Somiedo – Enramada Beech Forest loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route offers a good balance of challenge and scenic beauty for families with some hiking experience.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families in Somiedo?
A: Yes, Somiedo features several circular routes that are great for families, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For a moderate option that explores a beautiful beech forest, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40304363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Somiedo – Enramada Beech Forest loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt;. For more challenging circular options around the stunning glacial lakes, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lago de la Cueva – Lake of the Valley loop from Lagos de Saliencia&lt;/a&gt;, though these are rated as difficult.

Q: What natural features can families expect to see along the trails in Somiedo?
A: Families hiking in Somiedo will be captivated by its diverse natural beauty. You'll encounter impressive glacial lakes, such as the famous Lagos de Saliencia (including Lago de la Cueva and Lago del Valle), towering peaks, lush valleys, and ancient beech forests. The park's status as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve ensures a pristine natural experience.

Q: Can we spot wildlife while hiking with family in Somiedo?
A: Somiedo is a significant sanctuary for wildlife, making it an exciting place for families to observe nature. While sightings are never guaranteed, you might spot chamois, golden eagles, and griffon vultures. The park is also home to the Cantabrian brown bear, though bear sightings are rare and often require specialized tours. Always maintain a respectful distance from any wildlife you encounter.

Q: Are there any cultural landmarks or traditional structures to see on family hikes?
A: Absolutely! Somiedo is rich in ethnographic heritage. As you hike, you'll often come across 'brañas' – seasonal mountain pastures – and 'teitos,' which are unique ancient thatched-roof huts. These traditional constructions offer a fascinating glimpse into the region's historical mountain farming practices. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31858941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Valley – El Veneiru Teito Hut loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that specifically highlights these traditional structures.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit Somiedo for family hiking?
A: The best time for family hiking in Somiedo is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally milder, trails are clear of snow, and the landscapes are vibrant with flora. Summer offers warmer temperatures, while spring and autumn provide beautiful colors and fewer crowds.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family-friendly trails in Somiedo?
A: The family-friendly trails in Somiedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 440 ratings. Reviewers often praise the park's unspoiled nature, the breathtaking glacial lakes, and the tranquility that allows families to truly connect with the outdoors away from crowds.

Q: Is parking available near the family-friendly hiking trails in Somiedo?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near the starting points of many popular trails in Somiedo, especially in areas like Lagos de Saliencia or near villages such as Pola de Somiedo. It's advisable to check specific route details for precise parking information, particularly during peak season when spaces can fill up quickly.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to the famous glacial lakes, like Lago del Valle, suitable for families?
A: The glacial lakes are a highlight of Somiedo. While many routes to these lakes are challenging, active families can consider routes that lead to them. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Valley – View of Lago de la Cueva loop from Rio del Valle&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that takes you to Lago del Valle, the largest glacial lake in Asturias, traversing lush valleys and traditional 'brañas'.
tips:
This beautiful beech forest is located between the towns of El Coto de Buenamadre and Braña de Mumián.
Although it maintains its great beauty throughout the year, it is in autumn when it is in all its splendor with a great variety of colors.

The Braña de Mumián or Momián is one of the most important brañas in Somiedo.
The two easiest ways to get here are from El Llamardal or from the town of El Coto, crossing the beautiful beech forest of La Enramada.

This small stone church dedicated to Saint Cyprian is located on the outskirts of the town.
You can find the shield of the Order of Calatrava in the keystone of one of its chapels.
A few years ago the roof was restored.
